reviews.review_description:
I love diorama type stories in VR and this one is beautifully made. Very short but obviously made either a lot of love. Yes you’ll finish it quick but please encourage stories like this and don’t refund it if you enjoyed it. 
Can be played sitting or room scale

reviews.review_title: Not great if you are disabled
reviews.review_description:
I've updated my grade to a four just because the story is so charming. I really love the story. It's just difficult to navigate for certain people

I love everything about this except that, when you choose "seated" for your experience, they don't REALLY mean completely seated!  I have to crank this stupid handle thingy to get the ticket to come out but as the crank handle goes down, my hand is running into my legs (I'm in a wheelchair) and can't go any further!  It won't all these awards but none of them from the ADA.....

the Credits feel longer than the game which only lasts for 10minutes, so if you're interested in a shortstory it is worth it on deep sale, there is no gameplay, just pushing a button every 10-20 seconds or so

If you're searching for a traditional game, this is not the right choice. However, it's a beautiful story with a bit of interaction that makes it quite enjoyable. At this sale price, it's an easy decision for anyone who appreciates heartwarming tales. I highly recommend it!
Absolutely loved this story & experience, more please from the maker's.

The Line is a 16-minute Emmy Award-winning interactive story about breaking out of “The Line” i.e. comfort zone. It’s an immersive experience that takes you through the love story of two miniature dolls—Pedro and Rosa.
It’s set in a miniature diorama where viewers are invited to pull knobs and ring bells of the machinery to unfold the story of the dolls.
I would recommend this movie to people who want to explore the immersive experience that comes with VR.
Not much interaction

developer_response.body:
One of the main ideas when we developed The Line was to create something meaningful and impactful for people of any age to enjoy in a short time.
Regarding "not much interaction", as we state in our description "The Line is a 15-20 minute interactive story".
The Line is an experience, not a game per se, different from other games we have here at ARVORE, that are supposed to be hours long.
If you are looking forward highly interactive games we encourage you to try these other games.

reviews.review_description: It's a nice little story, but it only provides twen minutes of content. The user initiates but can't influence a few of the steps to the story, but the story, and the steps never change, so once it finishes, there is no point in repeating. It transitions from a quant, one time, ten minute experience, into hard drive space to erase so other programs can be loaded. How can Meta/Facebook/Oculus/whoever wrote this, charge $5 for a 10 minute experience? So disappointing. This is my first purchased program for my Quest, and if this is a worthy representation of the value of these programs then perhaps it's also my last  Meta/Facebook/Oculus purchase. Most popular social/meeting place VR apps are free, and mainstream commercial apps can be purchased through and run from Steam and my desktop Nvidia graphics card.

developer_response.body:
One of the main ideas, when we developed this experience, was to create something meaningful and impactful for everyone to enjoy in a short time. As we state in our description "The Line is a 15-20 minute interactive story".
The Line is an experience, not a game per se, different from other games we have here at ARVORE, that are supposed to be hours long. We encourage you however to try these other games of ours if you are looking for a game with longer gameplay.

reviews.review_description: Game story too short for the price. Story too short for any price. Something like this should be another tutorial game for VR. No options for the story at all. You get one choice only anytime user input is required.

developer_response.body:
One of the main ideas, when we developed this experience, was to create something meaningful and impactful for everyone to enjoy in a short time. As we state in our description "The Line is a 15-20 minute interactive story".
The Line is an experience, not a game per se, different from other games we have here at ARVORE, that are supposed to be hours long. We encourage you however to try these other games of ours if you are looking for a game with longer gameplay.

reviews.review_description:
I loved this VR award winning VR experience from the early launch on Quest. One of the first with hand tracking. The beautiful story is great to show first timers in VR how immersive VR is. Start with hands everyone can jump in. I showed my 4 years old granddaughter it 2 times 10 minutes in two days and the second play she could use all buttons and handles. ‘Pedro is so cute’ she said 😊 not scary at all.
Yes I know VR is not a daily game for toddlers, but it shows how great this game/experience is.

My 91-year oldgrandmother-in-law loves passive narrative experiences and I was looking for something with some very light interactions to give her a feel for what hand tracking can do. She really enjoyed this. Its a good intro for hand tracking interactions, and a pleasant little story. Yes its quite short but for this kind of demo to family, thats not reallh a bad thing

developer_response.body:
Wow, you don't know how special for us to read this review 💜

Yeah, The Line is supposed to be short since the concept days. One of the main ideas, when we developed this experience, was to create something meaningful and impactful, and just like you said, we reached that goal :)

Give Grandma a big hug from us 😊

reviews.review_description: After all the glowing reviews I thought I'd give it a go and wished I'd saved my money. The 'game' isn't even 30 minutes long and all you do is press whatever is lit after listening to the narration for a while. I was hoping for a little more interactivity and definitely a longer game for the money!

developer_response.body:
Hello Friend! We appreciate your feedback on The Line. One of the main ideas, when we developed this experience, was to create something meaningful and impactful for everyone to enjoy in a short time. As we state in our description "The Line is a 15-20 minute interactive story".
The Line is an experience, not a game per se, different from other games we have here at ARVORE, that are supposed to be hours long. We encourage you however to try these other games of ours if you are looking for a game with longer gameplay.

reviews.review_description: Short and sweet story

This was very cute. 

If developers are reading this, please make more content, and make them longer if possible 🙏🏼 This film shows just how much potential you all have. 

I appreciated the subtitles option, for accessibility reasons. A lot of other VR films aren’t doing their subtitles and it really rubs me the wrong way, as it completely excludes deaf people and the hard of hearing from enjoying. So, I was very happy to see that included with this film. 

Anyways, although it was cute, I was still left feeling cheated of some sort of content or entertainment. Perhaps the story needed more of an exciting/robust climax and plot? Despite such an interesting location and visual concept, the plot fell short for me. 

Loved the part when he fell through the set, and when he broke free from track…although I wish it would have been more dramatized and difficult for  him to breaking free so it would have been that much more satisfying when he did. I would have enjoyed it more if we could have seen inside buildings too, or more of the story shrunk down to their size to mix things up a bit and keep it interesting/us on our toes? It was just a little too slow and short for me to give all 5 stars.

This is a short (~20 minute) interactive VR story. Very simple but beautifully done and a nice message. Worth checking out for the price of Starbucks coffee, even if brief enough to feel like a demo of what a good VR experience should be. 

Can be played sitting or in room scale. I switched to the latter and recommend it as I found myself wanting to poke my head around to look closer at the details. Def more immersive that way!

reviews.review_title: A pint or a unique experience!
reviews.review_description:
For the price of a pint of beer that tastes like piss, you can have a unique and special experience, fully utilising the capabilities of the quest. 

People complaining it’s short… correct it is and I wish they had another story, but it’s under £4!
How do you think people make money to do more of these creative experiences!?

If you’re not a lemon buy it, if you’re looking for something to mindlessly throw hours into and can’t afford a pint then don’t. 

The ending was an incredible but simple message…  but no spoilers, just saying it’s well put together!

reviews.review_title: Wish I could get my money back
reviews.review_description: Barely interactive. Might as well be watching on your TV. This won an emmy??

Is this story short? Yes!  But for $5, I didn't expect anything more. 

In fact, I think the length is prefect. Get in, tell the story, get out, back to Beat Saber.

This is the app I show my friends who aren't gamers. They've all been very impressed.


To the people complaining about how there's not much interaction or gameplay... it's not the point of this app! It's not a game! Just sit back and enjoy a wonderfully told story.

reviews.review_title: 4 stars for content and 1 star for value
reviews.review_description: The content is cute. But keep in mind this has ZERO replay value. Buy it once, watch it, and that is it. I would have paid at most half of its cost. This is the last time I’m buying ultra low-interactive contents like this.

reviews.review_title: Dutch subtitles and voices would be nice
reviews.review_description: I’m a bit disappointed that this game supports so many European languages but doesn’t have any support for the Dutch language whatsoever. A fix for that would be appreciated.

I was excited to play this game when I hit play it won't open. I went to the store and pressed start it loaded for five ten seconds then next thing I knew I was at the store. I shut off the headset, got back on and the same thing happened again. Then I deleted it, and reinstalled it the same thing happened again. About the entire headset battery latter it worked. It was very stressful trying to open it I thought it would never work

developer_response.body:
Hi there friend, we're sorry to hear that. But don't worry, we'll help you with your issue.
First of all, can you please confirm to us which headset you're using? This will help us understand your case a little better.

If possible, could you also inform the specs of your PC?

reviews.review_description: The best way to be introduced to boundless opportunities of VR. A story that will make you rethink your choices.

Very beautiful.
I would like to have it in French!

developer_response.body:
We're glad you are enjoying The Line :)

About French support: Even though we have no French audio, we do have French as an option for our interface and subtitles!

reviews.review_description: No word to describe this experience
I was blown away by the reviews. But this is not for everyone. 10 minutes and you are done. Nice story but it doesn't live to the reviews that you see here.
Love the imagery and the narration.  You may only watch it once, but for the price it's worth it.  I will be showing this to my family/friends that are not familiar with VR.
Watching this story really made my day. It's short but wonderfully crafted. As a child I really believed that the Borrowers existed, and after this I'm sure that they do!
I really loved the story and the atmosphere, but it was a very short game as the price. It worths every penny, but I wanted to stay in the town more.

This is the most precious and adorable VR experience!  I had a look of child-like wonderment on my face the whole time. I didn’t want to leave this magical miniature world.  I’ll never forget this amazing interactive experience.
It is a short, simple love story, with an original setup and narrative. Graphics and design are beautiful and it is pleasing to spend some time inspecting every little detail. Very enjoyable.

It’s not something you would replay. Felt quite polished even though the length of the story was quite short. 

Hand tracking was for some simple triggers in the game, nothing complicated. 

I got it with my free credits :)

1) Claims to support seated play, chose seated from menu, control panels were below my body so buttons could not be pressed.

2) Re-centering via the Oculus controller does not recenter the game, you have to quit and restart.

Got so frustrated with a bunch of controls and buttons that were below my legs or belly which I could not reach.

Fell at the first hurdle and was unable to progress beyond the first minute of gameplay :(

Adding the simple ability to raise/lower/reposition the environment would have solved this.

For these reasons I found this title to be inaccessible and I just cannot recommend.  Real shame as it looks like it would have been cool.

developer_response.body:
Hello friend! We truly appreciate you supporting our studio by purchasing 'The Line' and taking the time to give us your feedback. It is our goal to make this experience as accessible as possible and we are certainly going to look into adding this feature in a future update. Currently, you can change the height by resetting your guardian floor level which will tell the application which height to set the table. We do hope that you will manage to see the experience that we worked so hard on. If you'd like to speak to us more you can join us on our discord here - https://discord.gg/8h8TAgX.
Again, thank you for sending us your feedback, it is very useful for us to hear about issues like this!

reviews.review_title: Very childish writing, not worth it
reviews.review_description: This may captivate a child's interest but for adults, there is really nothing too see here but a bunch of cliches and uninteresting story writing.

I really enjoyed this app. The story is cute.  It's short and there isn't a lot to do but there are buttons to push, levers to pull, knobs to turn and you are walking a little bit, a few steps now and again to get to the next part of the story.  You start by sitting on the floor and you end up down on the floor for a minute at another point as this is where the story takes you.  You can do this sitting down or standing up.  I stood up while the story played out and had no issues with balance.  

Some may take issue with paying money for such a short experience that they may only watch once but it is really cheap and I feel, it's worth it.  I actually ran through it a couple of times.  This is also something you can let someone else who maybe isn't familiar with VR try out for themselves.
Its only a short story with limited action on your part but its beautiful,i recommend

It is an extremely sweet and very short story. It was suggested as a hand tracking app but it isn't (or at least it didn't work for me).

I wished there was the possibility to take the characters in my hands or exploring the world after the story. It's not an impossible thing to make and it would add a lot to the interactivity.

I wish there were more of these. Absolutely fantastic narration and story and models, even the credits were beautiful

reviews.review_title: Super cute and well done but short.
reviews.review_description: I must say that it is really short, even for the low price. But it's super cute and really well done. The hand tracking works well and the fact that you can move in the play space is really neat! Would recommend even if it's short.

I went in not expecting much. But this was just so precious. It goes to show that VR doesn't have to be guns, cars, and immersive jump scares. There's plenty of room for an immersive story told from a unique perspective.The hand tracking interaction can occasionally be a little finicky (I'm looking at you crank organ), but overall this is just so... so... The only word I can think of to describe this is "precious". It's a sweet story and well worth getting, especially at it's price point.

1st Impressions Video with Commentary:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6Rwz03uSuI4

My Initial Thoughts:
A very cool and stylish diorama with story elements you can control and interact with your OWN hands in VR thanks to the power of hand tracking.

It was a beautiful game and the graphics were good for that kind if game. Although it was very pretty and meaningful...I think it could have been 2 dollars maybe 3 but not 5, I think it was a little too short to be 5 dollars but that's just my opinion. Anyways, a beautiful game, had a lot of meaning, and the graphics were great for this type of game. Bye

developer_response.body:
Hello Friend! We absolutely appreciate your feedback on The Line! We dedicated a lot of resources and time to research and develop this experience in order to create something meaningful and impactful for everyone. We do understand that the price may not suit some viewers, and we are doing our best to match these expectations with the ability to continue making projects in the future. As we are indie developers based in Brazil, your support for our projects is very much appreciated and we will continue to do our best!
Also, if you'd like to talk to us directly you can join our discord here - https://discord.gg/a2MzaeP or through email contact@arvore.io.

reviews.review_description: I love this but there is no real interaction. I think they should give this away for free
In my opinion there's not much replay value but it makes for a great experience to see hand tracking in action and to amaze your friends and family with it as well.

I wasnt expecting much given the price and overall simplified visuals but this little story has some real heart to it. Nicely animated with good sound design and narration. It's very short at maybe 10 minutes but definitely worth a revisit. The interaction in the game is very minor and could be removed with little to no effect either way. It added nothing to the experience for me personally. Overall, a quality experience that I am glad to support with my $5.

reviews.review_title: Worth it if you have discretionary money
reviews.review_description: If you’re short on cash I would save and buy a game, otherwise you won’t be disappointed.

reviews.review_title: Sweet but a one trick pony
reviews.review_description: For people learning blue to use their VR hands for the first time it’s great, but for VR veterans it’s a one time experience. However, it is very heartfelt and awesome experience.

Special poetic experience 
Loved it.
Not even a game a super short story

developer_response.body:
Hi friend! We do apologize if you felt that you were misled when purchasing our experience from the store. You are correct that this is more of a narrative experience and less of a game, as that was our purpose for the piece. We tried to include this info in the description by stating "15-20 minute interactive story", as well as including the genre as "Art/Creativity, Movie, Narrative".
Could you please help us understand how we can better distinguish our application to avoid confusion? We would love to hear your feedback! If you can reach out to us on our email contact@arvore.io or through our discord channel. (https://discord.gg/jRzeVac)
Thanks for your time! - team

reviews.review_description: Very beautiful slightly interactive story. But unfortunately too short.

Artistic and heart-warming
Too short tho...
very well done, cute fairy-tale story.

Ok. It's only a 15 minute ride. It says in the description, so stop wining about the €5 it costs.(going to a bar will also cost me €5 in 10 minutes, so what's to complain about) 
The dedication and work put into it are definitely worth it. Very beautiful story! It's an experience.
Btw..... Use roomscale while playing it. It's so engaging when you move from one part to another, sitting to standing,....totally immerges you into the setting.
I like how it makes you sit in the beginning of the game and the different ways it immerses you into the game. Great game for the price and great story!
A little bit too short but it’s expected with this price. Wishing a Chinese version then I can show it to my parents.
This is a great immersive experience!!  I enjoyed it.  My kids loved it.  Really well presented and thought out.  Thanks to the Devs for this little gem!!
Unique physical mechanics (stand up & sit down) provide yet another facet of how we can view and interact with VR media. The story is sweet, powerful, and loving. A great addition to the library.

reviews.review_title: Great story and great story telling!
reviews.review_description: Awesome, well laid out, written, and designed. Highly recommend.

reviews.review_title: 10 minutes later and I'm still smiling!
reviews.review_description: What an absolutely wonderful experience! Storytelling at it's best. The hand tracking work perfectly, the mobility kept me involved, and the ending "experience" made me a virtual reality fan for life. The narration by one of my favorite Westworld characters was also a nice touch. Excellent job to the development team. Keep these types of high caliber experiences coming and I'll be happy to pay a reasonable price for them.
Beautiful short story! :)
It's so cute, and such a great concept. I played through it twice, but...I wish there was more to it. I love the theme, I love the art, I love the gameplay. I just wish there was more game to play.
I would be grateful if you would make it in Arabic language!
